example.name: package_baw_artifacts
example.description: Package widgets, business objects, and coaches into a TWX artifact
example.context: Use when artifacts are ready and the goal is TWX generation

steps.step: Inspect the widgets requested for packaging
Check for standalone business objects in business-objects/generated/
Review the packaging configuration in package_baw.py
Report the generated artifact with template version used
Confirm all artifact types (widgets, business objects, coaches) are included

example.name: troubleshoot_packaging_failure
example.context: Use when packaging does not produce the expected TWX output

steps.step: Review the failed command output and packaging inputs
Check whether the issue is configuration-related or caused by widget readiness problems
Report whether the next action stays in packaging mode or requires widget-authoring handoff

example.name: validate_before_packaging
example.description: Perform a lightweight readiness review before running the build
example.context: Use when newly created or modified artifacts are about to be packaged

steps.step: Inspect the target widgets and metadata files
Check business objects in business-objects/generated/ for valid JSON structure
Check coaches in coaches/ for valid XML structure
Confirm the packaging configuration in package_baw.py aligns with available widgets
Proceed to packaging only if there are no obvious blockers

example.name: deploy_after_successful_packaging
example.description: Deploy a toolkit only after packaging has succeeded and deployment is requested
example.context: Use when a generated toolkit should be installed into a BAW environment

steps.step: Confirm the artifact path and deployment intent
Report installation status and any asynchronous follow-up required
